20.text: 1. After hearing argument on the appeal, the Court pronounced orders allowing the appeal, discharging the anti-suit injunction and upholding the Appellants’ objection to the jurisdiction of the Court ...

21.text: 2. Having undertaken that assessment, for the reasons which follow, the Appellants’ costs are quantifi ed and assessed in the amount of AED 720,000.

23.text: 3. The fi rst Statement of Costs fi led by the Appellants covered all work done up to and including the hearing of the return of the anti-suit injunction on 5 February 2025. That Statement was in the ...

24.text: 4. The second Statement of Costs fi led by the Appellants covered all work done between the hearing on 5 February 2025 up to and including the hearing of the appeal on 16 October 2025. The amount of t...

25.text: 5. The combined total of the two Statements is AED 759,731.93.

27.text: 6. During the course of the proceedings the Respondent has fi led six separate Statements of Costs which together total AED 1,316,016.06. While it is noted that the Respondent carried the burden of in...

28.text: the appeal and the objection to jurisdiction. Accordingly, the costs claimed by the Respondent provide a sense check in relation to the quantum of the costs claimed by the Appellants and suggest that ...

30.text: 7. The Respondent has raised a number of objections to the costs claimed by the Appellants. They will be considered in turn.

31.text: 8. First, the Respondent complains that the costs have not been adequately particularised. The complaint has two components. First it is said that the Statements of Costs do not differentiate between ...

32.text: 9. In relation to the fi rst aspect of the complaint, the Court has ordered the Respondent to pay the Appellants’ costs of the proceedings, without differentiation between particular components of the...

33.text: 10. In relation to the complaint relating to the disbursements to English solicitors and counsel, it is the usual practice in this Court for disbursements to counsel to be claimed as a lump sum, witho...

34.text: 11. The Respondent contends that the Appellants’ objection to jurisdiction was unnecessary and that the Appellants’ costs should be reduced accordingly. The Respondent contends that it would have been...

35.text: 12. This submission is rejected. The objection to jurisdiction was necessary and appropriate. Without such an objection, the success of the appeal would have resulted in the discharge of the injunctio...

36.text: 13. The Respondent further contends that the Appellants’ costs should be reduced because they include work done in applying for an extension of time to appeal from the dismissal of the objection to ju...

37.text: 14. The Respondent further contends that the costs claimed by the Appellants should be reduced proportionately because the decision of the Court of Appeal constituted a change in the law. This submiss...

38.text: 15. The submissions advanced in support of this contention repeat the arguments that were rejected by the Court of Appeal. For the reasons given by the Court of Appeal, neither Brookfield Multiplex v ...

39.text: 16. The short point, which the Respondent appears unwilling to accept, is that the legislative provisions empowering the Court to grant interim and permanent injunctive relief are sources of power to ...

40.text: 17. For the reasons given by the Court of Appeal, neither the proceedings nor the decision at fi rst instance were sustainable by reference to previous authority.

41.text: 18. The Respondent also contends that some of the costs incurred by the Appellants arose from errors made by the Court, including the inconsistency between the orders made by the Court and the reasons...

42.text: 19. It must be accepted that these are not matters for which the Respondent is directly responsible. However, it must also be accepted that they are not matters for which the Appellants were responsib...

43.text: 20. The Respondent further contends that the Appellants’ costs should be reduced because permission was refused in respect of some of the grounds of appeal which were proposed. That proposition should...

44.text: 21. The Respondent further contends that the fact that the Appellants obtained an anti-arbitration injunction from the English Court subsequent to the decision of the Court of Appeal is somehow releva...

46.text: 22. The costs claimed by the Appellants should be reduced taking into account that the costs claimed include costs incurred applying for an extension of time to appeal from the decision on the objecti...

47.text: 23. Taking these various matters into account, the Appellants’ costs are assessed in the amount of AED 720,000.
